questions.question: How does DeepSeek-V3 relate to the six-knob framework of Lesson 14?
options: It ignores the standard knobs entirely

It turns all six standard knobs (RMSNorm, RoPE, SwiGLU, pre-norm, weight tying, MoE) AND adds four of its own: Multi-Head Latent Attention, auxiliary-loss-free load balancing, Multi-Token Prediction, and DualPipe training
questions.explanation: Llama 3 405B is "GPT-2 with six knobs turned"; DeepSeek-V3 (671B total / 37B active) is the first frontier open model whose deep structure meaningfully diverges — and the blueprint many 2026 runs copy.

questions.question: How does MLA differ from GQA?
options: MLA uses more KV heads than GQA

GQA shares K/V across query-head groups; MLA compresses K and V into ONE shared low-rank latent per token per layer (kv_lora_rank ≈ 512 floats) and decompresses per head on the fly — the cache stores only the latent
MLA removes the KV cache entirely
questions.explanation: The up-projections can even be absorbed into subsequent matmuls. Extra decompression compute is small against the bandwidth saved — a net win for long-context inference.

questions.question: What is the MLA cache advantage at 128k context, by the numbers?
options: 61 layers × 512 latent × 131072 tokens × 2 bytes ≈ 7.6 GB, versus ~30.5 GB for a Llama-3-70B-style GQA baseline (2 × 61 × 8 heads × 128 dim) — 4x smaller
questions.explanation: Deriving these numbers from the published config is the lesson's core skill: the architecture choices become concrete serving dollars.

questions.question: Which part of the stack does each DeepSeek-specific innovation target?
options: MLA targets inference KV-cache memory; auxiliary-loss-free routing targets MoE training quality (no balance penalty polluting the objective); MTP targets training signal density plus free speculative drafts; DualPipe targets multi-node training utilization
questions.explanation: Four innovations, four different bottlenecks — memory, objective purity, supervision density, and pipeline bubbles. That breadth is why the model defined a new frontier recipe.

questions.question: Why is the 671B-total / 37B-active ratio "the right bet"?
options: Capacity (knowledge, specialization) scales with total parameters at storage cost while per-token compute scales only with active parameters — MoE + MLA together give frontier capability at a fraction of dense inference cost, which neither achieves alone
questions.explanation: MoE attacks the FLOPs bill, MLA attacks the memory bill — the two binding constraints of serving. Their combination is the architectural thesis of the model.
